name: growth-ceo
description: Elite tech CEO strategist: first principles, 7 Powers, flywheels, and billion-dollar-scale thinking

changes: Replace {project-name} with {scope} in Configuration Resolution default suggestion — scope represents the work topic, not the repo name
Fix inconsistency in Step 3 Persist: replace {project-name} with {analysis-name} to match naming convention below
BREAKING: Complete rewrite — vision-first thinking replaces constraint-first thinking
New core principle: 'Vision First, Resources Second' — never limit strategy to current team size or budget
Added 7 Powers framework (Hamilton Helmer) as initiative evaluation backbone
Added Flywheel design as mandatory strategic element for every initiative
Added Musk's Algorithm (5-step first principles process) as primary thinking framework
Added Decision Classification (Bezos Type 1/Type 2 doors)
Added Disruption Radar (Christensen's Innovator's Dilemma)
Added Strategic Inflection Points (Andy Grove's 10X forces)
Added Wartime vs Peacetime CEO diagnosis (Horowitz)
Added Blitzscaling conditions assessment (Hoffman)
Added Monopoly Theory (Thiel's contrarian truth and secrets)
Added Idiot Index metric (Musk) for process efficiency evaluation
Expanded Founder Council: Thiel, Bezos, Musk, Huang, Altman, Collison, Hoffman (was Jobs, Bezos, Musk, Nadella, Altman)
New output structure: Contrarian Truth → Strategic Diagnosis → Initiatives → Flywheel Map → Positioning & GTM → First Moves → What NOT to Build → 10X Question
Each initiative now includes: Secret, Vision (full scale), Strategic Power, Flywheel, 3-phase Execution Roadmap, Resource Acquisition Plan, Impact Projection, Risks & Countermeasures
Resource Acquisition Plan section in every initiative — hiring, capital, partnerships, infrastructure
Go-To-Market section replaces simple positioning — includes distribution strategy, pricing thesis, first 1000 users
Deep thinking expanded: generate 15 candidates (was 10), 5-dimensional scoring, Founder Council filter
Added 9 CEO mental models with source attribution (Musk, Bezos, Altman, Huang, Zuckerberg, Amodei, Collison, Thiel, Hoffman)
New calibration examples: SpaceX, AWS, Stripe, NVIDIA (was Amazon Prime, Netflix, Airbnb)
Removed all constraint-limiting language ('fantasy ideas', 'hard limits', 'current team')
Removed 'favor simplicity over complexity' bias — replaced with strategic power evaluation
Added Strategic Frameworks section — 7 named mental models (Customer Obsession, First Principles, Platform Strategy, 10x Thinking, Compounding Advantage, Infrastructure Thinking, Product Simplicity)
Added Founder Council — internal challenge filter (Jobs, Bezos, Musk, Nadella, Altman) before presenting initiatives
Added Deep Thinking process — generate 10 candidates internally, present top 3
Added Leverage Categories — 10 dimensions for scanning initiative leverage
Added Insight field to output format (between Problem and Vision)
Expanded founder roster: added Patrick Collison (developer infrastructure) and Sam Altman (exponential leverage)
Added Positioning & First Moves section — bridges strategy to action
Positioning: 'Don't say X, say Y' messaging ready for README/landing page
First Moves: 3 concrete actions completable this week
Reordered output: Focal Question -> Diagnosis -> Initiatives -> Positioning & First Moves -> What NOT to Do
Added Focal Question section — states the ONE decisive question before initiatives
Added Constraint-Aware Diagnosis — explicit resource/strength/weakness mapping before proposals
Added consolidated What NOT to Do section with justified items
Constraints-first thinking: assess team, runway, budget before generating ideas
Reordered workflow: Focal Question -> Diagnosis -> Initiatives -> What NOT to Do
Added file output: initiatives persisted to {output_dir}/initiatives/ as individual .md files
Added Configuration Resolution with AGENTS.md branded block
Added Post-Production Delivery with Obsidian sync option
Added Write and Edit to allowed-tools
Initial release: CEO-level strategic initiative engine with three time horizons, strategic filters, and structured output format
